title: pelargonium zonale
intro: Pelargonium zonale, also known as the zonal geranium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae, characterized by its rounded leaves with distinctive zones or markings. The leaves and flowers of the Pelargonium zonale plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Pelargonium zonale are often infused in teas, used in tinctures, or made into topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
